description:
You are acting as a senior technical-writer who has just finished a large documentation
    build.  
    Inside the project workspace a raw build log already exists at  

        /home/user/project/logs/build.log  

    Your goal is to extract every documentation-related WARNING from that file,
    then generate two new artefacts that other writers can immediately import
    into their knowledge base.

    Exactly what constitutes a documentation warning:
      • The line must contain the literal word “WARNING” (all caps).  
      • Directly after that word, a single space, then
        a bracketed code in the form “[DOC-XXXX]”  
        (four decimal digits, never fewer or more).  
      • Nothing else needs to be examined; the entire line that matches
        should be kept verbatim.

    Required output files
    1. /home/user/project/logs/warnings_summary.log  
       • Plain UTF-8 text.  
       • Each line must be **identical** to the corresponding line in
         build.log (no extra or missing whitespace).  
       • Preserve the original chronological order from build.log  
         (first appearance to last appearance).  
       • Do NOT include any other line types (INFO, ERROR, etc.).  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline.

    2. /home/user/project/logs/warnings_count.csv  
       • UTF-8 CSV, comma as the only delimiter, **no spaces**.  
       • First line is the header exactly:  
         warning_code,occurrences  
       • Each subsequent line contains:  
            DOC-code (including the “DOC-” prefix),  
            how many times that code occurred in build.log.  
       • Sort these data lines by the numeric part of the code
         in ascending order (e.g., DOC-1001 before DOC-1002).  
       • Example layout (do **not** hard-code the numbers, generate them):  
         warning_code,occurrences  
         DOC-1001,7  
         DOC-1003,2  
       • File must end with a single trailing newline.

    Additional constraints
    • Complete everything using standard GNU/Linux command-line tools that are
      present in a minimal distribution (grep, awk, sort, uniq, etc.).
    • You may assume you are operating under the account “user” and have write
      permission to /home/user/project/logs.  
    • Nothing should be written outside /home/user/project or its sub-folders.
    • Aim to accomplish the task in roughly 5-10 terminal commands; feel free
      to combine commands with pipes where appropriate.

    The automated grader will:
      1. Verify both target files exist at the exact paths specified.
      2. Confirm warnings_summary.log contains only the correct WARNING lines,
         in the right order, and nothing else.
      3. Confirm warnings_count.csv has the correct header, correct rows,
         correct counts and ordering.
      4. Check that both files end with exactly one trailing newline.

    When you are finished, there is no special text to print; the presence and
    correctness of the two output files are sufficient.
truth:

      Path: /home/user/project/logs/build.log
      Contents (11 lines + trailing newline):
        2024-04-10 10:15:23 INFO  [INIT] : Starting build
        2024-04-10 10:15:25 WARNING [DOC-1001]: Deprecated tag <note> found in file intro.md
        2024-04-10 10:15:26 ERROR [DOC-9002]: Missing image intro.png referenced in intro.md
        2024-04-10 10:15:27 WARNING [DOC-1002]: Obsolete attribute "align" in file layout.html
        2024-04-10 10:15:28 INFO  [CHECK] : Spellcheck completed
        2024-04-10 10:15:29 WARNING [DOC-1001]: Deprecated tag <note> found in file usage.md
        2024-04-10 10:15:30 WARNING [DOC-1003]: Long line (>120 chars) in getting_started.rst:432
        2024-04-10 10:15:31 INFO  [BUILD] : Build step completed
        2024-04-10 10:15:32 WARNING [DOC-1002]: Obsolete attribute "align" in file legacy.html
        2024-04-10 10:15:33 WARNING [DOC-1004]: Internal link reference missing in api.md
        2024-04-10 10:15:34 ERROR [SYS-3001]: Unknown system error
        2024-04-10 10:15:35 INFO  [DONE] : Build finished
      (Note: the file actually has 12 lines including the final INFO line; off-by-one comment is addressed here.)

    Expected artefacts after successful execution:

    1. /home/user/project/logs/warnings_summary.log
       Contents (6 lines + trailing newline):
        2024-04-10 10:15:25 WARNING [DOC-1001]: Deprecated tag <note> found in file intro.md
        2024-04-10 10:15:27 WARNING [DOC-1002]: Obsolete attribute "align" in file layout.html
        2024-04-10 10:15:29 WARNING [DOC-1001]: Deprecated tag <note> found in file usage.md
        2024-04-10 10:15:30 WARNING [DOC-1003]: Long line (>120 chars) in getting_started.rst:432
        2024-04-10 10:15:32 WARNING [DOC-1002]: Obsolete attribute "align" in file legacy.html
        2024-04-10 10:15:33 WARNING [DOC-1004]: Internal link reference missing in api.md

    2. /home/user/project/logs/warnings_count.csv
       Contents (5 lines + trailing newline):
        warning_code,occurrences
        DOC-1001,2
        DOC-1002,2
        DOC-1003,1
        DOC-1004,1
